info.name: Claude API — Full Surface
info.description:
# Claude API — Complete reference collection

Every public endpoint of the Anthropic Claude API at `https://api.anthropic.com`, cross-checked against the live docs sitemap (platform.claude.com, 2026-06-11).

## How to use

1. **Import the matching environment file** (`claude-api.postman_environment.json`) and select it.
2. Fill in `anthropic_api_key`. For Admin / Compliance endpoints also fill `anthropic_admin_api_key` (starts with `sk-ant-admin-...`).
3. As you create resources, paste the returned ids back into the environment (e.g. set `agent_id` after **Managed Agents → Agents → Create agent**).

## Folder map

| # | Folder | Auth | Beta header |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Messages | API key | — |
| 2 | Message Batches | API key | — |
| 3 | Files (beta) | API key | `files-api-2025-04-14` |
| 4 | Models | API key | — |
| 5 | Managed Agents (beta) | API key | `managed-agents-2026-04-01` |
| 6 | Skills (beta) | API key | `skills-2025-10-02` |
| 7 | Deployments (beta) | API key | `managed-agents-2026-04-01` |
| 8 | Deployment Runs (beta) | API key | `managed-agents-2026-04-01` |
| 9 | User Profiles (beta) | API key | `managed-agents-2026-04-01` |
| 10 | Admin API | **Admin** key | — |
| 11 | Compliance API | **Admin** key | — |

## Conventions

- Folder-level descriptions explain the workflow. Each request has its own description with payload notes and gotchas.
- All bodies use the recommended modern shapes (adaptive thinking, `output_config.format`, etc.). `temperature`/`top_p` are intentionally absent — they 400 on Opus 4.7+.
- Path placeholders are environment variables (`{{agent_id}}`, `{{session_id}}`, …). After a create call returns an id, paste it into the environment.
- Streaming requests carry `Accept: text/event-stream`. Postman renders the SSE stream live.

## Beta header overlaps

Some endpoints take resources from a different beta surface (e.g. `files.list?scope_id={session_id}` needs BOTH `files-api-2025-04-14` and `managed-agents-2026-04-01`). Where relevant the request comments out the secondary header — uncomment if you need it.

item.name: 1. Messages

request.description: Single-turn request. The minimum required body: `model`, `max_tokens`, and a `messages` array.
Set persona/behavior via the top-level `system` field. Keep it stable across requests for prompt caching to work.
Adaptive thinking is the only thinking mode on Opus 4.7+. `display: "summarized"` opts back into visible reasoning. `effort` controls thinking depth + overall token spend.
Server-Sent Events stream. Required for `max_tokens` > ~16K to avoid SDK HTTP timeouts. Postman renders incoming events live.
Define tools via JSON Schema. Response will contain `tool_use` blocks when the model wants to call a tool.
Continue the loop: echo the assistant's response (including `tool_use` blocks) and provide a `tool_result` in a new user turn. Replace `toolu_REPLACE_ME` with the id from the previous response.
Pass an image via URL. Also supports `source.type: "base64"` with `media_type` + `data` fields.
Mark a stable prefix with `cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}`. Verify hits via `usage.cache_read_input_tokens` on the response.
`output_config.format` with a JSON Schema constrains the response to valid JSON. Replaces the deprecated `output_format` parameter.
Accurate per-model token count. Don't use tiktoken — it undercounts Claude by 15-20%. Pass the same `model` you'll use for inference.

item.description: The Messages API — Claude's primary inference endpoint. POST `/v1/messages` with a `messages` array; the model returns content blocks (text, tool_use, thinking).

item.name: 2. Message Batches

request.description: Submit up to 100K requests at 50% of standard price. Most batches finish within 1 hour, max 24h. Returns a `msgbatch_...` id.
Poll until `processing_status` is `ended`. Counts are in `request_counts.{succeeded,errored,processing,canceled,expired}`.
Streams JSONL — one result per request, keyed by `custom_id`. Available once the batch ends; results stay for 29 days.
Paginated. `before_id`/`after_id` cursors. `limit` 1-1000.
Best-effort cancellation. In-progress requests within the batch may still complete; status moves to `canceling` then `ended`.
Delete only after the batch has ended. Removes results too.

item.description: Async batch processing at 50% of standard price. Submit up to 100K requests per batch; poll until ended; fetch JSONL results.

item.name: 3. Files (beta)

formdata.description: Pick a file via Postman's form-data UI.

request.description: Multipart upload. Max 500 MB. Returns a `file_id` to reference in `messages` content blocks or as a session resource. Set the file via Postman's form-data picker.
Paginated. Use `?scope_id={{session_id}}` (with managed-agents beta header) to list files written by a session to `/mnt/session/outputs/`.
Returns `filename`, `mime_type`, `size_bytes`, `created_at`. Does not return content.
Only files created by code execution, skills, or session outputs are downloadable — user uploads are not.
Removes the file from your org's quota.

item.description: Upload files for reuse across requests. Beta header `files-api-2025-04-14` is set per-request.

item.name: 4. Models

request.description: Auto-paginating list of available models. Each entry includes `id`, `display_name`, `created_at`.

Full capability tree: `max_input_tokens`, `max_tokens`, and `capabilities.{image_input, thinking, effort, structured_outputs, context_management, ...}`. Use this for runtime capability discovery instead of hardcoding.

item.description: Discover available models and their capabilities. Use Retrieve for live capability lookup instead of hardcoding.

item.name: 5. Managed Agents (beta)
Agents

request.description: Persisted, versioned agent config. `model`/`system`/`tools` live HERE — not on the session. Returns `id` (e.g. `agent_...`) + numeric `version`. Save both for later session creation.
Paginated.
Returns the agent's current configuration and latest version number.
Creates a NEW immutable version of this agent. Running sessions keep their pinned version; new sessions get the latest unless they pin explicitly.
PERMANENT — agent becomes read-only, new sessions cannot reference it, no unarchive. Existing sessions continue to run.
History of every config update. Use a specific version when creating a session for reproducibility.

item.description: Persisted, versioned agent configurations. **Required first step** before creating any session — `model`/`system`/`tools` live here.

item.name: Sessions

request.description: Start a session referencing a pre-created agent + environment. The `agent` field takes either a bare id string (latest version) or `{type:"agent", id, version}` (pinned).
Returns a paginated list of sessions. Path: `GET /v1/sessions`.
Returns `status` (idle | running | rescheduling | terminated), `usage`, `resources`, `agent` snapshot.
Updatable: `title`, `metadata`, plus session-local overrides of `agent.tools` / `agent.mcp_servers` / `vault_ids`. Session must be `idle`.
Permanently deletes the session, event history, container, and checkpoints.
Session becomes read-only. Not reversible.

item.description: One stateful run of an agent. References a pre-created agent and environment.

item.name: Session Events

request.description: Paginated full history. Use this on reconnect to consolidate against the live stream.
Append a user message. Queued FIFO; session transitions idle → running.
Reply to an `agent.custom_tool_use` event. Replace `sevt_REPLACE_ME` with the `id` from that event.
Approve or deny a built-in tool call under `always_ask` permission policy. `tool_use_id` is the originating `agent.tool_use` event's id.
Jump the queue. Forces the agent to a safe boundary then idle.
Start a rubric-graded iterate loop. `rubric` is `{type:"text", content}` or `{type:"file", file_id}`. The harness iterates until satisfied, hits `max_iterations`, fails, or is interrupted.
Long-lived SSE stream. Open BEFORE sending the kickoff event or you'll miss early events. No replay on disconnect — combine with `List events` for lossless reconnect.

item.description: The conversation API. Send `user.*` events; receive `agent.*` / `session.*` / `span.*` events via stream or list.

item.name: Session Threads

request.description: Multiagent only. Each subagent runs in its own thread with isolated context.
Carries `agent` snapshot, `status`, `parent_thread_id`, `stats`, `usage`.
Permanently marks as read-only thread. Path: `POST /v1/sessions/{{session_id}}/threads/{{thread_id}}/archive`.
Per-thread event history.
Per-thread SSE stream — only this subagent's events. The session-level stream gets a condensed view of all threads.

item.description: Per-subagent event streams in multiagent sessions.

item.name: Session Resources

request.description: Mount a previously-uploaded file into the session container at an absolute path. Files mount read-only.
Clone a GitHub repo at session start. `authorization_token` is never placed in the container — it's injected by an Anthropic-side git proxy.
Returns a paginated list of resources. Path: `GET /v1/sessions/{{session_id}}/resources`.
Returns one resource. Path: `GET /v1/sessions/{{session_id}}/resources/{{resource_id}}`.
Rotate the `authorization_token` for a mounted GitHub repo without recreating the session.
Permanently deletes resource. Path: `DELETE /v1/sessions/{{session_id}}/resources/{{resource_id}}`.

item.description: Files and repos mounted into the session container. Memory stores attach via `resources[]` on session-create only (not addable here).

item.name: Environments

request.description: Anthropic-hosted sandbox template. `networking.type` is `unrestricted` or `limited` (with `allowed_hosts`/`allow_package_managers`/`allow_mcp_servers`). Names must be unique within the workspace.
Tool execution runs on YOUR worker via outbound polling. `networking`/`packages` don't apply. Needs an environment key generated in Console.
Returns a paginated list of environments. Path: `GET /v1/environments`.
Returns one environment. Path: `GET /v1/environments/{{environment_id}}`.
Changes apply to NEW containers only; existing sessions keep their original config.
Returns 204.
PERMANENT — read-only. Existing sessions continue; new sessions cannot reference it.

item.description: Sandbox templates. Cloud (Anthropic-hosted) or self-hosted (your own worker).

item.name: Environment Work Queue (self-hosted)

request.description: Queue depth, pending items, workers polling. Use to monitor self-hosted worker health.
Paginated list of work items in the queue (queued / claimed / completed).
Self-hosted worker calls this to claim the next work item. Use the SDK's `EnvironmentWorker.run()` for normal cases — this is the raw endpoint.
`GET /v1/environments/{{environment_id}}/work/{{work_id}}`.
Worker tells the queue it's still alive; prevents reclaim. Call periodically while a tool is executing.
Acknowledge that the worker finished the item successfully.
Update the work item's state (progress, partial results).
Stop a claimed work item. Control-plane call (use `x-api-key`, not the environment key).

item.description: Self-hosted worker control plane. Most consumers use `EnvironmentWorker.run()` from the SDK — these are the raw HTTP endpoints.

item.name: Vaults

request.description: Container for MCP credentials. Anthropic auto-refreshes stored OAuth tokens. Attach via `vault_ids` on session create.
Returns a paginated list of vaults. Path: `GET /v1/vaults`.
Returns one vault. Path: `GET /v1/vaults/{{vault_id}}`.
Updates vault. Path: `POST /v1/vaults/{{vault_id}}`.
Permanently deletes vault. Path: `DELETE /v1/vaults/{{vault_id}}`.
Permanently marks as read-only vault. Path: `POST /v1/vaults/{{vault_id}}/archive`.

item.description: Containers for MCP credentials. Attach to sessions via `vault_ids`.

item.name: Credentials

request.description: Store a refreshable MCP OAuth credential. `refresh.token_endpoint_auth` is a discriminated union: `none` | `client_secret_basic` | `client_secret_post`.
Returns a paginated list of credentials. Path: `GET /v1/vaults/{{vault_id}}/credentials`.
Returns metadata only — secrets are write-only.
Updates credential. Path: `POST /v1/vaults/{{vault_id}}/credentials/{{credential_id}}`.
Permanently deletes credential. Path: `DELETE /v1/vaults/{{vault_id}}/credentials/{{credential_id}}`.
Permanently marks as read-only credential. Path: `POST /v1/vaults/{{vault_id}}/credentials/{{credential_id}}/archive`.
Test that the credential's `access_token` can authenticate against the MCP server URL.

item.description: MCP credentials stored inside a vault. Anthropic auto-refreshes OAuth tokens.

item.name: Memory Stores

request.description: Workspace-scoped persistent memory. Mount into a session via `resources: [{type: "memory_store", memory_store_id, access}]`. Max 8 per session.
Returns a paginated list of memory stores. Path: `GET /v1/memory_stores`.
Returns one memory store. Path: `GET /v1/memory_stores/{{memory_store_id}}`.
Updates memory store. Path: `POST /v1/memory_stores/{{memory_store_id}}`.
Permanently deletes memory store. Path: `DELETE /v1/memory_stores/{{memory_store_id}}`.
PERMANENT — read-only, no unarchive.

item.description: Workspace-scoped persistent memory. Mount into a session as a `memory_store` resource.

item.name: Memories

request.description: Returns `Memory | MemoryPrefix` entries. `path_prefix` scopes; `depth` bounds tree walk. `view=full` includes content.
Create at `path`. 409 (`memory_path_conflict_error`) if the path is occupied. Max 100KB per memory.
Defaults to `view=full` (includes content).
Mutate by `mem_...` id. Change `content`, `path` (rename), or both. Optional `precondition` for optimistic concurrency.
Optional `expected_content_sha256` for conditional delete.

item.description: Individual text files inside a memory store. Max 100KB per memory.

item.name: Memory Versions

request.description: Newest-first immutable snapshots. Filter by `memory_id`, `operation`, `session_id`, `api_key_id`, `created_at_{gte,lte}`.
Returns one memory version. Path: `GET /v1/memory_stores/{{memory_store_id}}/memory_versions/{{memory_version_id}}`.
Scrubs content + path while preserving actor/timestamps. Use for leaked secrets, PII, deletion requests.

item.description: Immutable per-mutation snapshots. Audit and rollback surface.
Stateful, server-managed agents. Beta header `managed-agents-2026-04-01` is set on every request.

item.name: 6. Skills (beta)

request.description: Create a custom skill. Upload the actual content via Create skill version (next folder).
Returns a paginated list of skills. Path: `GET /v1/skills`.
Returns one skill. Path: `GET /v1/skills/{{skill_id}}`.
Permanently deletes skill. Path: `DELETE /v1/skills/{{skill_id}}`.

item.name: Skill Versions

request.description: Upload a ZIP archive containing SKILL.md and supporting files. Use Postman's form-data picker to attach.
Returns a paginated list of skill versions. Path: `GET /v1/skills/{{skill_id}}/versions`.
Returns one skill version. Path: `GET /v1/skills/{{skill_id}}/versions/{{skill_version}}`.
Download the version's archive back. Returns the bytes.
Permanently deletes skill version. Path: `DELETE /v1/skills/{{skill_id}}/versions/{{skill_version}}`.

item.description: Each version is a separate uploaded archive.
Custom skill definitions. Beta header `skills-2025-10-02`.

item.name: 7. Deployments (beta)

request.description: A reusable triggerable agent invocation: pair an agent with an environment and a schedule/trigger. Returns `deployment_id`.
Returns a paginated list of deployments. Path: `GET /v1/deployments`.
Returns one deployment. Path: `GET /v1/deployments/{{deployment_id}}`.
Updates deployment. Path: `POST /v1/deployments/{{deployment_id}}`.
Trigger the deployment on demand. Returns a `deployment_run_id` and creates a session.
Disable scheduled runs without deleting the deployment.
Resume scheduled runs.
PERMANENT — read-only.

item.description: Triggerable scheduled or event-driven agent invocations. Beta header `managed-agents-2026-04-01`.

item.name: 8. Deployment Runs (beta)

request.description: History of triggered runs. Filter by `deployment_id`.
Returns the run's status and the associated `session_id` for drilling into events.

item.description: Per-trigger history of a deployment. Beta header `managed-agents-2026-04-01`.

item.name: 9. User Profiles (beta)

request.description: Represent an end-user of your agent app for per-user memory/vault scoping and usage attribution.
Returns a paginated list of user profiles. Path: `GET /v1/user_profiles`.
Returns one user profile. Path: `GET /v1/user_profiles/{{user_profile_id}}`.
Updates user profile. Path: `POST /v1/user_profiles/{{user_profile_id}}`.
Generate a short-lived URL the end-user opens to attach their own MCP credentials / OAuth to their profile.

item.description: Per-end-user state for multi-tenant agent apps. Beta header `managed-agents-2026-04-01`.

item.name: 10. Admin API

request.description: The org tied to your Admin API key. Returns `id`, `name`.
Returns a paginated list of API keys. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/api_keys`.
Returns one API key. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/api_keys/{{api_key_id}}`.
Mutate `name`, `status` (active/inactive/archived), or `workspace_id`.
Organization members. Filter by `email`.
Returns one user. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/users/{{user_id}}`.
Updates user role. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/users/{{user_id}}`.
Permanently removes user from org. Path: `DELETE /v1/organizations/users/{{user_id}}`.
Returns a paginated list of invites. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/invites`.
Creates a new invite. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/invites`.
Returns one invite. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/invites/{{invite_id}}`.
Permanently deletes invite. Path: `DELETE /v1/organizations/invites/{{invite_id}}`.
Returns a paginated list of workspaces. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/workspaces`.
Creates a new workspace. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/workspaces`.
Returns one workspace. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/workspaces/{{workspace_id}}`.
Updates workspace. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/workspaces/{{workspace_id}}`.
PERMANENT.
Returns a paginated list of workspace members. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/workspaces/{{workspace_id}}/members`.
Adds to workspace member. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/workspaces/{{workspace_id}}/members`.
Returns one workspace member. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/workspaces/{{workspace_id}}/members/{{user_id}}`.
Updates workspace member role. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/workspaces/{{workspace_id}}/members/{{user_id}}`.
Permanently removes workspace member. Path: `DELETE /v1/organizations/workspaces/{{workspace_id}}/members/{{user_id}}`.
Org-level rate limits per model/tier.
Workspace-scoped overrides of org rate limits.
Token consumption broken down by model, workspace, service tier.
Per-user daily activity: sessions, lines of code, commits, PRs, tool usage. Data older than 1h only.
Cost in USD by model/workspace/tier.
External keys for federated/BYOK setups.
Creates a new external key. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/external_keys`.
Returns one external key. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/external_keys/{{external_key_id}}`.
Updates external key. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/external_keys/{{external_key_id}}`.
Permanently deletes external key. Path: `DELETE /v1/organizations/external_keys/{{external_key_id}}`.
Verify the key works against its target.
MCP tunnels expose self-hosted MCP servers to managed agents through an Anthropic-side proxy.
Returns one MCP tunnel. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/mcp_tunnels/{{mcp_tunnel_id}}`.
Permanently marks as read-only MCP tunnel. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/mcp_tunnels/{{mcp_tunnel_id}}/archive`.
One-time reveal of the tunnel's connection token.
`POST /v1/organizations/mcp_tunnels/{{mcp_tunnel_id}}/rotate_token`.
Returns a paginated list of tunnel certificates. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/mcp_tunnels/{{mcp_tunnel_id}}/tunnel_certificates`.
Creates a new tunnel certificate. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/mcp_tunnels/{{mcp_tunnel_id}}/tunnel_certificates`.
Returns one tunnel certificate. Path: `GET /v1/organizations/mcp_tunnels/{{mcp_tunnel_id}}/tunnel_certificates/{{certificate_id}}`.
Permanently marks as read-only tunnel certificate. Path: `POST /v1/organizations/mcp_tunnels/{{mcp_tunnel_id}}/tunnel_certificates/{{certificate_id}}/archive`.

item.description: Organization management. **Requires an Admin API key** (`sk-ant-admin-...`) — set `anthropic_admin_api_key` in the environment. This folder overrides the collection auth to use that key.

item.name: 11. Compliance API

request.description: Audit log entries across the org.
Returns a paginated list of projects. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/projects`.
Returns one project. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/projects/{{project_id}}`.
Permanently deletes project. Path: `DELETE /v1/compliance/apps/projects/{{project_id}}`.
Returns a paginated list of project attachments. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/projects/{{project_id}}/attachments`.
Returns a paginated list of project documents. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/projects/{{project_id}}/documents`.
Returns one project document. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/projects/{{project_id}}/documents/{{document_id}}`.
Returns project document metadata. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/projects/{{project_id}}/documents/{{document_id}}/metadata`.
Permanently deletes project document. Path: `DELETE /v1/compliance/apps/projects/{{project_id}}/documents/{{document_id}}`.
Returns a paginated list of chats. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats`.
Permanently deletes chat. Path: `DELETE /v1/compliance/apps/chats/{{chat_id}}`.
Returns a paginated list of chat messages. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/{{chat_id}}/messages`.
Returns a paginated list of chat files. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/{{chat_id}}/files`.
Returns one chat file. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/{{chat_id}}/files/{{file_id}}`.
Returns the binary contents of chat file. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/{{chat_id}}/files/{{file_id}}/download`.
Permanently deletes chat file. Path: `DELETE /v1/compliance/apps/chats/{{chat_id}}/files/{{file_id}}`.
Returns one generated file. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/{{chat_id}}/generated_files/{{file_id}}`.
Returns the binary contents of generated file. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/chats/{{chat_id}}/generated_files/{{file_id}}/download`.
Returns one artifact. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/artifacts/{{artifact_id}}`.
Returns the binary contents of artifact. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/apps/artifacts/{{artifact_id}}/download`.
Returns a paginated list of groups. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/groups`.
Returns one group. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/groups/{{group_id}}`.
Returns a paginated list of group members. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/groups/{{group_id}}/members`.
Returns a paginated list of organizations. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/organizations`.
Returns a paginated list of org users. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/organizations/{{compliance_org_id}}/users`.
Returns a paginated list of org roles. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/organizations/{{compliance_org_id}}/roles`.
Returns one org role. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/organizations/{{compliance_org_id}}/roles/{{role_id}}`.
Returns a paginated list of role permissions. Path: `GET /v1/compliance/organizations/{{compliance_org_id}}/roles/{{role_id}}/permissions`.

item.description: Enterprise audit / export endpoints for claude.ai usage. Requires an Admin API key (same as Admin API).
